import anndata as ad
import scanpy as sc

# ----------------------------
# 4) Build AnnData
# ----------------------------
adata = ad.AnnData(X=X, obs=obs.copy())
adata.var_names = [f"dim_{i}" for i in range(X.shape[1])]
adata.obs_names = adata.obs["obs_name"].tolist()

print(adata)
adata.obs.head()

​AnnData object with n_obs × n_vars = 25000 × 1024
    obs: 'layer', 'position', 'token_id', 'token', 'token_text', 'obs_name'

# ----------------------------
# 6) Print observations by cluster
# ----------------------------
cluster_sizes = adata.obs["leiden"].value_counts()

print("\nCluster summary:")
for cl in sorted(cluster_sizes.index, key=int):
    subset = adata.obs[adata.obs["leiden"] == cl]
    size = subset.shape[0]
    preview = subset[["layer", "position", "token_text"]].head(12)
    print(f"\nCluster {cl} (size={size})")
    print(preview.to_string(index=False))

import pandas as pd

# ----------------------------
# 3) Stack all layers together
# ----------------------------
# hidden_states[0] is embedding output
# hidden_states[1:] are transformer block outputs
include_embedding = True

layer_states = hidden_states if include_embedding else hidden_states[1:]
layer_offset = 0 if include_embedding else 1

input_ids = inputs["input_ids"][0].detach().cpu().tolist()
tokens = tokenizer.convert_ids_to_tokens(input_ids)
token_texts = [
    tokenizer.decode([tid], clean_up_tokenization_spaces=False)
    for tid in input_ids
]

rows = []
obs_rows = []

for i, h in enumerate(layer_states):
    layer_index = i + layer_offset
    X_layer = h[0].to(torch.float32).numpy()
    rows.append(X_layer)

    for pos, (tid, tok, tok_text) in enumerate(zip(input_ids, tokens, token_texts)):
        obs_rows.append({
            "layer": layer_index,
            "position": pos,
            "token_id": tid,
            "token": tok,
            "token_text": tok_text,
            "obs_name": f"L{layer_index}_P{pos}",
        })

X = np.vstack(rows).astype(np.float32)
obs = pd.DataFrame(obs_rows)

print("Joint activation matrix shape:", X.shape)
print("Number of layers included:", len(layer_states))
print("Sequence length:", len(tokens))

​Joint activation matrix shape: (25000, 1024)
Number of layers included: 25
Sequence length: 1000

inputs = tokenizer(text, return_tensors="pt")
inputs = {k: v.to(device) for k, v in inputs.items()}

with torch.no_grad():
    outputs = model(**inputs, output_hidden_states=True)

# move hidden states back to CPU so later code is simpler
hidden_states = tuple(h.detach().cpu() for h in outputs.hidden_states)

print(f"Number of hidden-state tensors: {len(hidden_states)}")
for i, h in enumerate(hidden_states[:3]):
    print(f"hidden_states[{i}].shape = {tuple(h.shape)}")

​Number of hidden-state tensors: 25
hidden_states[0].shape = (1, 1000, 1024)
hidden_states[1].shape = (1, 1000, 1024)
hidden_states[2].shape = (1, 1000, 1024)

import requests

# ----------------------------
# 1) Download text
# ----------------------------
url = "https://www.gutenberg.org/files/11/11-0.txt"
response = requests.get(url)
full_text = response.text

# ----------------------------
# 2) Clean Gutenberg header/footer
# ----------------------------
def clean_gutenberg(text):
    start = text.find("*** START")
    end = text.find("*** END")
    if start != -1 and end != -1:
        return text[start:end]
    return text

clean_text = clean_gutenberg(full_text)

# ----------------------------
# 3) Tokenize and take a chunk
# ----------------------------
tokens = tokenizer(clean_text, return_tensors="pt", truncation=False)
input_ids = tokens["input_ids"][0]

# skip intro (optional but recommended)
start = 500
length = 1000   # reduce if you hit memory issues

chunk_ids = input_ids[start:start + length]

# ----------------------------
# 4) Decode back to text
# ----------------------------
text = tokenizer.decode(chunk_ids, clean_up_tokenization_spaces=False)

print("Final text length (tokens):", len(chunk_ids))
print(text[:500])  # preview

​Final text length (tokens): 1000
 down a
large rabbit-hole under the hedge.

In another moment down went Alice after it, never once considering how
in the world she was to get out again.

The rabbit-hole went straight on like a tunnel for some way, and then
dipped suddenly down, so suddenly that Alice had not a moment to think
about stopping herself before she found herself falling down a very
deep well.

Either the well was very deep, or she fell very slowly, for she had
plenty of time as she went down to look about her and to

import torch
import numpy as np

model_name = "Qwen/Qwen3.5-0.8B"   # or another model if available

from transformers import AutoModel, AutoTokenizer

tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(model_name, trust_remote_code=True)
model = AutoModel.from_pretrained(model_name, trust_remote_code=True)

device = "cuda" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu"
model = model.to(device)
model.eval()

print("device:", device)
​device: cuda

nodes.title: HSV-generated palette
widgets_values:
import colorsys
import random
import matplotlib.colors as mcolors

def make_distinct_colors(n, saturation=0.65, value=0.75, seed=42):
    rng = random.Random(seed)
    hues = [i / n for i in range(n)]
    rng.shuffle(hues)
    colors = []
    for h in hues:
        r, g, b = colorsys.hsv_to_rgb(h, saturation, value)
        colors.append(mcolors.to_hex((r, g, b)))
    return colors

def get_text_color(bg_color):
    r, g, b = mcolors.to_rgb(bg_color)
    brightness = 0.299 * r + 0.587 * g + 0.114 * b
    return "black" if brightness > 0.6 else "white"
print(adata.obs["leiden"].value_counts().describe())

import html # --------------------------------------- # Config # --------------------------------------- start_pos = 11 n_tokens_to_show = 20 layer_stride = 4 # --------------------------------------- # Build HTML visualization for ALL layers # --------------------------------------- all_layers_full = sorted(adata.obs["layer"].unique()) all_layers = all_layers_full[::layer_stride] # ensure last layer is included if all_layers_full[-1] not in all_layers: all_layers.append(all_layers_full[-1]) clusters_all = adata.obs["leiden"].astype(str) try: unique_clusters = sorted(clusters_all.unique(), key=int) except ValueError: unique_clusters = sorted(clusters_all.unique()) # Use HSV-generated distinct colors colors = make_distinct_colors( len(unique_clusters), saturation=0.8, value=0.55, seed=1, ) cluster_to_color = {} for cl, color in zip(unique_clusters, colors): cluster_to_color[cl] = color # ---------------------------- # CSS # ---------------------------- css_lines = [ "") css = "\n".join(css_lines) # ---------------------------- # HTML body # ---------------------------- layer_blocks = [] for layer in all_layers: adata_layer = adata[adata.obs["layer"] == layer] token_texts = adata_layer.obs["token_text"].tolist() clusters = adata_layer.obs["leiden"].astype(str).tolist() token_texts = token_texts[start_pos:start_pos + n_tokens_to_show] clusters = clusters[start_pos:start_pos + n_tokens_to_show] spans = [] for tok, cl in zip(token_texts, clusters): safe_tok = html.escape(tok) bg = cluster_to_color[cl] fg = get_text_color(bg) spans.append( f"{safe_tok}" ) block = f"""
Layer {layer}
{''.join(spans)} ...
""" layer_blocks.append(block) # ---------------------------- # Legend # ---------------------------- legend_parts = ["
"] for cl in unique_clusters: color = cluster_to_color[cl] fg = get_text_color(color) legend_parts.append( f"{cl}" ) legend_parts.append("
") legend_html = "\n".join(legend_parts) # ---------------------------- # Final HTML # ---------------------------- html_text = f""" {model_name} all layers {css}

{model_name} – All Layers (tokens {start_pos} to {start_pos + n_tokens_to_show - 1}, every {layer_stride} layers)

{legend_html} {''.join(layer_blocks)} """ Result = html_text

# ----------------------------
# 5) Build graph, cluster, UMAP with Scanpy
# ----------------------------
sc.pp.neighbors(
    adata,
    n_neighbors=30,
    use_rep="X",
    metric="cosine",
)

sc.tl.leiden(
    adata,
    resolution=0.2,
)

sc.tl.umap(adata)

print(adata)
print("UMAP shape:", adata.obsm["X_umap"].shape)
print(adata.uns["neighbors"])

​AnnData object with n_obs × n_vars = 25000 × 1024
    obs: 'layer', 'position', 'token_id', 'token', 'token_text', 'obs_name', 'leiden'
    uns: 'neighbors', 'leiden', 'umap'
    obsm: 'X_umap'
    obsp: 'distances', 'connectivities'
UMAP shape: (25000, 2)
{'connectivities_key': 'connectivities', 'distances_key': 'distances', 'params': {'n_neighbors': 30, 'method': 'umap', 'random_state': 0, 'metric': 'cosine', 'use_rep': 'X'}}

import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

plt.rcParams["figure.facecolor"] = "#cccccc"

# ----------------------------
# Config
# ----------------------------
base_size = 8
highlight_size = 28
base_alpha = 0.75
highlight_alpha = 0.5
highlight_color = "black"

adata_sub = adata.copy()
adata_sub.obs["leiden"] = adata_sub.obs["leiden"].astype("category")

# rebuild Leiden colors locally
clusters_all = adata_sub.obs["leiden"].astype(str)
try:
    unique_clusters = sorted(clusters_all.unique(), key=int)
except ValueError:
    unique_clusters = sorted(clusters_all.unique())

colors = make_distinct_colors(
    len(unique_clusters),
    saturation=0.8,
    value=0.55,
    seed=1,
)

cluster_to_color = {
    cl: color for cl, color in zip(unique_clusters, colors)
}

adata_sub.uns["leiden_colors"] = [
    cluster_to_color[c] for c in adata_sub.obs["leiden"].cat.categories
]

if "X_umap" not in adata_sub.obsm:
    raise ValueError("UMAP not found in adata.obsm['X_umap']")

X_umap = adata_sub.obsm["X_umap"]
layers = sorted(adata_sub.obs["layer"].unique())

# fixed limits for animation consistency
x = X_umap[:, 0]
y = X_umap[:, 1]
pad_x = 0.05 * (x.max() - x.min())
pad_y = 0.05 * (y.max() - y.min())
xlim = (x.min() - pad_x, x.max() + pad_x)
ylim = (y.min() - pad_y, y.max() + pad_y)

# base colors from Leiden
leiden_codes = adata_sub.obs["leiden"].cat.codes.to_numpy()
leiden_categories = list(adata_sub.obs["leiden"].cat.categories)
base_colors = [
    cluster_to_color[leiden_categories[i]] for i in leiden_codes
]

for layer in layers:
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 10))
    fig.patch.set_facecolor("#cccccc")
    ax.set_facecolor("white")

    # base plot: original Leiden-colored UMAP
    ax.scatter(
        X_umap[:, 0],
        X_umap[:, 1],
        c=base_colors,
        s=base_size,
        alpha=base_alpha,
        linewidths=0,
        rasterized=True,
    )

    # highlight current layer by overdrawing it
    mask = (adata_sub.obs["layer"].to_numpy() == layer)

    # # --- outer white ring ---
    # ax.scatter(
    #     X_umap[mask, 0],
    #     X_umap[mask, 1],
    #     c="white",
    #     s=highlight_size * 1.5,   # bigger than inner dot
    #     alpha=1.0,
    #     linewidths=0,
    #     zorder=3,
    #     rasterized=True,
    # )

    # --- inner black dot ---
    ax.scatter(
        X_umap[mask, 0],
        X_umap[mask, 1],
        c="black",
        s=highlight_size * 1,
        alpha=highlight_alpha,
        linewidths=0,
        zorder=4,
        rasterized=True,
    )


    ax.set_title(f"UMAP – highlight layer {layer}", fontsize=16)
    ax.set_xlim(xlim)
    ax.set_ylim(ylim)
    ax.set_xticks([])
    ax.set_yticks([])

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

plt.rcParams["figure.facecolor"] = "#cccccc"

sc.set_figure_params(figsize=(10, 10))

adata_sub = adata.copy()

# ensure categorical
adata_sub.obs["leiden"] = adata_sub.obs["leiden"].astype("category")

# --- rebuild colors (safe, no dependency on other cells) ---
clusters_all = adata_sub.obs["leiden"].astype(str)

try:
    unique_clusters = sorted(clusters_all.unique(), key=int)
except ValueError:
    unique_clusters = sorted(clusters_all.unique())

colors = make_distinct_colors(
    len(unique_clusters),
    saturation=0.8,
    value=0.55,
    seed=1,
)

cluster_to_color = {
    cl: color for cl, color in zip(unique_clusters, colors)
}

adata_sub.uns["leiden_colors"] = [
    cluster_to_color[c] for c in adata_sub.obs["leiden"].cat.categories
]

# --- IMPORTANT: ensure UMAP exists ---
if "X_umap" not in adata_sub.obsm:
    raise ValueError("UMAP not found in adata.obsm['X_umap']")

# --- plot ---
sc.pl.umap(
    adata_sub,
    color=["leiden", "layer"],
    size=16,
)

target = "down"

for layer_id in range(1,24):
    cluster_id = "10"

    mask_cluster = adata.obs["leiden"].astype(str) == cluster_id
    mask_I = adata.obs["token_text"].str.strip() == target
    mask_layer = adata.obs["layer"] == layer_id

    # combine all conditions
    mask_highlight = mask_cluster & mask_I & mask_layer

    X_umap = adata.obsm["X_umap"]

    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 8))

    # all cluster 15 points (background)
    ax.scatter(
        X_umap[mask_cluster, 0],
        X_umap[mask_cluster, 1],
        s=14,
        alpha=0.25,
        c="lightgray",
        label=f"cluster {cluster_id}",
    )

    # highlight only "I" from layer 0
    ax.scatter(
        X_umap[mask_highlight, 0],
        X_umap[mask_highlight, 1],
        s=50,
        alpha=0.9,
        c="red",
        label=f"'I' (layer {layer_id})",
    )

    ax.set_title(f"UMAP – cluster {cluster_id}, highlight 'I' (layer {layer_id})")
    ax.set_xlabel("UMAP1")
    ax.set_ylabel("UMAP2")
    ax.set_xticks([])
    ax.set_yticks([])
    ax.legend()

    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()
    Notebook.add_plot()
    # break

window = 5

df = adata.obs.copy()
mask = df["token_text"].str.strip() == target

for _, row in df[mask].iterrows():
    layer = row["layer"]
    if layer>0:
        break
    pos = row["position"]

    df_layer = df[df["layer"] == layer]

    context = df_layer[
        (df_layer["position"] >= pos - window) &
        (df_layer["position"] <= pos + window)
    ].sort_values("position")

    tokens = []
    for _, r in context.iterrows():
        tok = r["token_text"]
        if r["position"] == pos:
            tok = f"[{tok.strip()}]"  # highlight
        tokens.append(tok)

    print(f"\nPos {pos}")
    print("".join(tokens))
